claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your circumstances.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To start, I need to ask you about the child or children you're inquiring about:

**Do you have a child or children you're responsible for? If yes, how old are they?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes, I'm asking about my child Alex. He's 15 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Alex is 15 years old, which means he's under 16. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at all. He's still in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits himself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex in any kind of apprenticeship program?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. He's just doing his GCSEs at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Do you live with Alex, or do you contribute to his upkeep (such as paying for food, clothes, housing, or other costs)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, such as another parent or guardian?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex currently in any of these situations: in hospital for a long stay, in local authority care, or being fostered?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those. He just lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- He's not working 24 hours or more per week
- He's not receiving benefits in his own right
- He's not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- You normally live in the UK
- Alex lives with you at home
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- The local council is not paying towards his accommodation or maintenance

**Next steps to claim:**
1. Apply online or by post using form CH2
2. You'll need to provide proof of identity for yourself and Alex
3. You'll need Alex's birth certificate or adoption certificate
4. Payments are typically made every 4 weeks

**Important note:** If you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge, which reduces or cancels out the benefit through the tax system. However, you can still claim Child Benefit even if this applies, as it protects your National Insurance credits.

Would you like any more information about the claiming process?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's great, thank you so much for your help! I have all the information I need now.
